prompt_text: This is my natural speaking voice, calm, clear, and steady, as I read these few lines aloud today.
text: This is a cloned version of my voice, created by a computer.
asr_check: This is a cloned version of my voice, created by a computer.
notes: First successful zero-shot clone. v2 base collapsed (premature EOS); v2Pro + cut0 fixed it.

prompt_text: This is my natural speaking voice, calm, clear, and steady, as I read these few lines aloud today.
text: I understand that rejection hurts, and rejection makes you feel ways that you really don't want to feel.
asr_check: I understand that rejection hurts and rejection makes you feel ways that you really don't want to feel.
notes: Longer two-clause sentence; v2Pro + cut0 + temp 0.7 produced full line cleanly.

speaker: danil
prompt_text: This is my natural speaking voice, calm, clear and steady. As I read these few lines aloud today, I speak at an even pace with
text: I love Germany, and my Russian husband, Mak Seem.
intended_text: I love Germany and my Russian husband Maxime.
asr_check: I love Germany and my Russian husband Maxine.
notes: First danil zero-shot clone. Ref trimmed to 9s (v2Pro requires 3-10s). 'Maxime' needs phonetic respelling; 'Mak Seem' gives mak-SEEM stress (best). Name is the only tricky token; rest of sentence clean.

speaker: danil
text: Hello — this is a quick zero-shot test of the cloned voice, reading a couple of natural English sentences aloud.
notes: Direct Python zero-shot (get_conditioning_latents + inference, language en, temp 0.7). torch 2.11.0+cu128, transformers 4.57.6, torchcodec 0.11.1+cu128. Gotchas fixed: transformers pinned <=4.57.6; torch>=2.6 weights_only -> add_safe_globals([XttsConfig,XttsAudioConfig,XttsArgs,BaseDatasetConfig]); torchcodec needed nvidia-npp-cu12 (libnppicc.so.12) on LD_LIBRARY_PATH via env activate.d. post: loudnorm I=-16 TP=-1.5.

speaker: danil
text: Hello — this is a quick zero-shot test of the cloned voice, reading a couple of natural English sentences aloud.
notes: Zero-shot via F5TTS.infer(ref_file, ref_text, gen_text), nfe_step=32, cfg=2.0, seed=42. ref_text = danill_ref9 transcript. torch 2.11.0+cu128, transformers 5.9.0, torchcodec 0.11.1+cu128. Gotchas: torchcodec needed nvidia-npp-cu12 (libnppicc.so.12) on LD_LIBRARY_PATH via env activate.d (same as xtts). F5 has flexible torch pin (>=2.0.0) so cu128 torch not downgraded. post: loudnorm I=-16 TP=-1.5.

speaker: danil
text: Hello — this is a quick zero-shot test of the cloned voice, reading a couple of natural English sentences aloud.
notes: Zero-shot clone via ChatterboxTTS.from_pretrained(cuda) + generate(text, audio_prompt_path=ref), exaggeration=0.5, cfg_weight=0.5, temp=0.8. torch 2.11.0+cu128 (OVERRIDE: pkg pins torch==2.6.0 which lacks sm_120; installed --no-deps then deps minus torch), transformers 5.2.0, numpy 1.26.4. No torchcodec in this env -> saved via soundfile (torchaudio.save routes through torchcodec in torch 2.11). pip prints a benign torch==2.6.0 version-conflict warning; runtime works on sm_120. post: loudnorm I=-16 TP=-1.5.

speaker: danil
prompt_text: This is my natural speaking voice, calm, clear and steady. As I read these few lines aloud today, I speak at an even pace with
text: Hello — this is a quick zero-shot test of the cloned voice, reading a couple of natural English sentences aloud.
asr_check: Hello, this is a quick zero shot test of the clone voice, reading a couple of natural English sentences aloud.
notes: Regenerated on the SHARED test sentence (matching 004/005/006) for a fair cross-engine A/B; scored by experiments/eval_zeroshot.py.
